pi_items.source: Code analysis: export_result.sh line 14, setup_task.sh confirms user 'ga' and directory structure
Code analysis: export_result.sh line 15, setup_task.sh confirms directory structure

Biological verification: HeLa nucleus diameter 9.6±2 µm (lower bound ~7.6 µm). With 1.0 µm/pixel spacing (specified in task), minimum 5 pixels is reasonable for smaller nuclei or partial measurements. Cross-referenced with similar task (stack_reslicing_analysis) which uses same pixel spacing convention.

Biological verification: HeLa cells exhibit nuclear size variation. Standard diameter 9.6±2 µm (~12 pixels max at 1.0 µm/pixel). However, HeLa cells can undergo polyploidy under stress conditions, producing polyploid giant cancer cells with nuclei up to 10x larger (reaching 50-60 µm diameter, or 50-60 pixels). This maximum accounts for normal variation plus polyploid variants. Consistent with comparative task (zstack_depth_profiling) where Fly Brain has 57-pixel depth.

privileged_info_summary: The task involves measuring HeLa cell nucleus Z-axis depth using ImageJ Reslice operation with 1.0 micron pixel spacing. Expected outputs are CSV and TIFF files saved to /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/. The metadata claim that nucleus depth ranges from 5-60 pixels is biologically justified: HeLa nuclei have diameters of 9.6±2 µm (translating to ~8-12 pixels at 1.0 µm/pixel), with maximum values accounting for polyploid variants that can reach 50-60 µm diameter under stress conditions.
